success_surface: Format-preserving column addition with conditional value population
effective_behaviors: Combined pandas and openpyxl for optimal data processing and format preservation
Used standard library copy() for openpyxl style object cloning instead of direct assignment
Wrapped style copying operations in try/except for graceful degradation
Explicitly copied column dimensions when adding new columns
Validated results with row counts and conditional logic summaries
evidence: First attempt failed with TypeError due to StyleProxy direct assignment; second fix used copy() from standard library
Third fix imported copy from copy module successfully, resolving ImportError

memory_items.title: OpenPyXL Style Cloning Mechanism
memory_items.lesson: When copying cell formatting attributes (font, border, alignment, fill, number_format) in openpyxl, always use copy() from Python's standard library, not direct assignment or openpyxl imports.
memory_items.generalization: StyleProxy objects require shallow copying before modification or assignment to avoid TypeError.

memory_items.title: Defensive Formatting Operations
memory_items.lesson: Wrap style copying operations in try/except blocks to allow partial success even if specific style elements fail.
memory_items.generalization: Complex object assignments in spreadsheet libraries benefit from error isolation to maintain execution continuity.

memory_items.title: Mixed-Mode Spreadsheet Processing
memory_items.lesson: Use pandas for data transformations and logic, then switch to openpyxl for writing values while preserving existing formatting.
memory_items.generalization: Separate computational tasks from presentation tasks across appropriate libraries.

memory_items.title: Column Dimension Synchronization
memory_items.lesson: When inserting new columns, explicitly set their width to match adjacent columns for visual consistency.
memory_items.generalization: New structural additions should inherit dimensional properties from surrounding context.

memory_items.title: Result Validation Logging
memory_items.lesson: Always log operation metrics (row counts, condition matches, skipped entries) upon completion.
memory_items.generalization: Quantitative verification supports debugging and confirms task scope coverage.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Style Object Handling**: When copying cell formats in openpyxl, use `copy()` from Python's standard library (`from copy import copy`), not direct assignment. StyleProxy objects require shallow cloning.
- **Formatting Robustness**: Wrap style attribute assignments in try/except blocks to prevent single failures from breaking entire row processing.
- **Column Width Propagation**: After adding new columns, explicitly set their width to match neighboring columns (`column_dimensions[col].width = column_dimensions[adjacent_col].width`).
- **Mixed-Mode Workflow**: Perform logical transformations with pandas, then write final values via openpyxl to retain original workbook styles and layouts.
- **Execution Verification**: Log quantitative results (total rows processed, conditionally matched entries, exceptions caught) to confirm correct scope coverage.
